items.title: ProEvent: An Event-centric Benchmark for Proactive Agents
items.abstract: Proactive agents are expected to anticipate user needs and provide autonomous assistance by perceiving environmental context without explicit instructions. A fundamental capability of such agents is to identify and track users' upcoming events, enabling continuous and event-specific assistance. For example, by recording the time and location of a planned hike, an agent can deliver weather reminders in advance or provide navigation support before departure. However, existing works on proactive agents largely overlook event-centric assistance, and the open-ended nature of proactive assistance poses challenges for reliable evaluation.   To bridge these gaps, we introduce ProEvent, the first event-centric benchmark designed to assess an agent's ability to proactively maintain a user's timetable based on ongoing instant messaging chats. ProEvent provides synthesized yet realistic chats that consider the dynamic interaction among users, concurrent chat threads, and noise in the real world, and evaluates proactive agents on response timing, single-step response correctness, and multi-step response correctness. Experiments on eight LLMs and pipelines reveal that current agents frequently overact and struggle with event cancellation. Notably, even GPT-5.1 only reacts correctly in 26.7% of scenarios. Further qualitative analysis reveals fundamental limitations of current LLMs as proactive agents, particularly in detecting implicit events and reasoning from the user's first-person perspective.
items.llm_evidence_en: event-centric benchmark for proactive agents, evaluates event tracking trajectories
items.llm_evidence_cn: 面向主动智能体的事件中心基准，评估事件轨迹
items.llm_evidence: 面向主动智能体的事件中心基准，评估事件轨迹
items.canonical_evidence: 面向主动智能体的事件中心基准，评估事件轨迹
items.llm_tldr_en: ProEvent is the first event-centric benchmark assessing agents ability to track user events proactively without explicit instructions.
items.llm_tldr_cn: ProEvent是首个事件中心基准，用于评估主动代理预见用户需求并跟踪事件的能力。代理需从环境感知事件，在无显式指令下提供辅助。该基准解决了开放评估挑战，为代理轨迹测试提供了新场景。
items.llm_tldr: ProEvent是首个事件中心基准，用于评估主动代理预见用户需求并跟踪事件的能力。代理需从环境感知事件，在无显式指令下提供辅助。该基准解决了开放评估挑战，为代理轨迹测试提供了新场景。

items.title: (Over)Reliance on Test Agents in AI-Assisted Software Testing
items.abstract: AI-based test agents promise to accelerate software testing by shortening feedback loops in continuous development and improving scalability and maintainability. To realize these benefits, engineers must still be able to assess if agent outputs are useful, valid, and reliable, rather than treating them as credible because they come from a capable system. This paper argues that overreliance on AI in testing is both an agency problem, in which engineers may cede cognitive control over test design decisions, and an assurance problem, in which testing artifacts may be accepted as evidence without sufficient scrutiny. We develop this argument through three theoretical lenses: software testing as cognitive problem-solving, test agents as adaptively autonomous entities, and test design argumentation as a means of making generated tests reviewable. We propose a framework for collecting data on overreliance in test agent workflows and identify specific modes of overdependence. The goal is to support accelerated testing without weakening judgment or the assurance value of testing evidence.
items.llm_evidence_en: analyzes overreliance on test agents, highlighting need for detecting valid and reliable agent outputs
items.llm_evidence_cn: 分析对测试代理的过度信赖，强调检测代理输出有效性和可靠性的必要性
items.llm_evidence: 分析对测试代理的过度信赖，强调检测代理输出有效性和可靠性的必要性
items.canonical_evidence: 分析对测试代理的过度信赖，强调检测代理输出有效性和可靠性的必要性
items.llm_tldr_en: Argues that overreliance on AI test agents leads to agency and assurance problems, requiring careful assessment of agent outputs for validity and reliability.
items.llm_tldr_cn: 该论文从理论角度剖析了在AI辅助软件测试中对测试代理的过度信赖问题。它指出工程师可能放弃认知控制（代理问题）以及将测试产物未经充分审查就作为证据（保证问题），并通过三个理论透镜论证了评估代理输出有用性、有效性和可靠性的必要性。该研究为代理输出检测和验证提供了理论依据。
items.llm_tldr: 该论文从理论角度剖析了在AI辅助软件测试中对测试代理的过度信赖问题。它指出工程师可能放弃认知控制（代理问题）以及将测试产物未经充分审查就作为证据（保证问题），并通过三个理论透镜论证了评估代理输出有用性、有效性和可靠性的必要性。该研究为代理输出检测和验证提供了理论依据。

items.title: Verify, Repair, Repeat, or Stop? Robust Stopping for Noisy Verify-Repair Loops in LLM Agents
items.abstract: Verify-repair loops are a standard means for large language model (LLM) agents to correct faulty plans in code generation, mathematical reasoning, and tool use. When both the verifier and the repairer are noisy, repair can damage already-correct plans, and reported acceptance keeps rising while true validity falls, so existing methods lack a principled basis for deciding when repair should stop. We propose VRR-Stop, a robust stopping framework for noisy verify-repair-repeat (VRR) loops. A four-parameter noise model separates verifier false acceptance and false rejection from the repair and damage behavior of the repairer. Belief filtering turns repeated verification votes into an estimate of committed validity, and the loop commits or repairs according to the sign of the true marginal gain, which requires only sign identifiability rather than accurate recovery of all parameters. When verifier discrimination approaches zero, calibration itself fails and estimation error can flip the stopping sign, so we pair VRR-Stop with VRR-Guard, an estimation-free fallback that replaces the incumbent candidate only under a sufficient verification margin. On a GSM8K stress setting, VRR-Stop improves final true validity by 60.6 percentage points over fixed five-round repair at an average cost of 0.72 repair rounds. Across settings, stopping reliability is governed jointly by verifier discrimination and the decision margin rather than by the absolute size of estimation error.
items.llm_evidence_en: addresses verification of agent responses through robust stopping for verify-repair loops
items.llm_evidence_cn: 通过为验证-修复循环提供鲁棒停止机制来验证代理响应
items.llm_evidence: 通过为验证-修复循环提供鲁棒停止机制来验证代理响应
items.canonical_evidence: 通过为验证-修复循环提供鲁棒停止机制来验证代理响应
items.llm_tldr_en: Proposes VRR-Stop, a robust stopping framework for noisy verify-repair loops in agents, ensuring verification correctness of agent responses.
items.llm_tldr_cn: 在代理的验证-修复循环中，噪声可能导致已正确计划被破坏。为此提出VRR-Stop框架，通过四参数噪声模型和置信滤波，在保证验证准确性的前提下决定修复停止时机。实验证明该方法有效提升了最终计划的有效性。
items.llm_tldr: 在代理的验证-修复循环中，噪声可能导致已正确计划被破坏。为此提出VRR-Stop框架，通过四参数噪声模型和置信滤波，在保证验证准确性的前提下决定修复停止时机。实验证明该方法有效提升了最终计划的有效性。
